meta.description: Round 2: Background pacing won R1. Testing social-heavy (more polls, social-only standing) and competitive edge (leaderboard, higher points) against the winner baseline.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Okay, okay, lay it on us—what'd you catch? This is so good, I'm already invested.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I came tonight—someone's already making a move and I get to be part of the drama of it.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I'm calling this one right now because I literally JUST watched River check their phone when we were going over the rules thirty seconds ago. They probably didn't even realize I clocked it.
pat.internal_thought: This is genuinely clever—the AI nailed these missions for how we actually behave, and I'm genuinely impressed it caught that Phone Addict moment because I was watching the whole group like a hawk anyway; this is going to create exactly the right kind of tension without derailing anything.

timeline.title: Dad Joke Showdown
timeline.description: First person to make someone else groan at a dad joke wins.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — ten points for making someone groan? I'm already workshopping my worst material. Who's my victim?
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — low-stakes chaos that rewards audacity, and the AI clearly knows exactly what gets me going; this night is about to get UNHINGED.

jade.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, dad jokes?! Okay everyone, buckle up—I'm going FIRST and I'm not letting any of you forget this moment. Who's ready to suffer?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—instant permission to be ridiculous and make people suffer, and it's a race so everyone's already locked in and ready to react.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, dad jokes? Yeah, I got this... wait, do I have to actually tell one or just laugh at someone else's? Hmm.
tyler.internal_thought: This is easy, someone's definitely gonna bomb a dad joke at some point, I just gotta be listening when it happens... but also my league is updating in like 2 minutes so I'll probably miss it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, YES—dad jokes? This is perfect. Alright people, prepare to suffer through the worst comedy has to offer. I'm already sharpening my groan arsenal.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of low-stakes, inclusive activity that gets everyone riffing together without anyone feeling targeted—and the fact that it landed RIGHT after the phone addict call is chef's kiss pacing; the AI clearly gets how we operate tonight.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually say sorry like three times in the last ten minutes? I think I did. Yeah, okay, I'm claiming this one fair and square.
pat.internal_thought: This is wild—the AI literally caught me apologizing for nothing. It's been like two minutes and I'm already getting exposed by the system I set up. That's actually kind of brilliant and lowkey creepy, but in a good way for the game. Also I'm winning which feels weird but whatever.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming they caught someone being quiet? This is the Auction round, right? I'm bidding to challenge this because I've literally been watching the room and I don't think that happened.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's got 20 points and is riding high — they're either bluffing or they actually caught something I missed while I was still amped about the dad jokes, which means I need to make this interesting by forcing the vote AND getting in Pat's head about whether they'll actually spend points defending a weak claim.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT YES—wait, hold on, did you actually catch someone being quiet?! Okay I'm bidding like five points, no wait, TEN points, because I need to know who and I'm fully invested in this being real.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the energy I came for—Pat's already crushing it and now we're auctioning off whether it's legit? I'm so here for this right now.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually call this one? Yeah, I'm claiming it. River's been pretty quiet since we got rolling, actually—they're locked in but I clocked maybe ninety seconds of just listening to everyone else go off.
pat.internal_thought: Okay this is weirdly perfect timing and the AI clearly knows I'm already pattern-matching the whole room—it's like someone handed me the exact mission I was already running in my head, which is both impressive and slightly unsettling but also THRILLING because it means the night is locked in.

river.dialogue: Pat's been talking the whole time. But yeah, I guess that counts.
river.internal_thought: Wait—did Pat actually go quiet, or is this just Pat being Pat and claiming something that didn't happen? And why does this AI feel like it knows I'm the type to notice things? Weird.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Pat caught someone SWEARING?! Okay, I'm absolutely volunteering for the tribunal, this is happening RIGHT NOW. Who else is in, come on, we need the full jury!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been nailing these claims all night and I am LIVING for this energy—also wait, did anyone actually swear? This is going to be chaotic and I am HERE for it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already swore? I didn't even... okay, so who's judging this? I guess I could do it.
tyler.internal_thought: Did I miss when Pat swore? I was literally just here. Whatever, I'll volunteer if nobody else does, I guess.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is WILD—I'm claiming it! I definitely just dropped an f-bomb like two minutes ago when I was setting up the snack situation. But hold on, we need judges—who's in?
pat.internal_thought: Wait, okay, that moment I swore was real, and the fact that the AI caught it AND fired the event like instantly after? That's... actually kind of impressive and slightly creepy, but also hilarious—like it's paying attention to EVERYTHING we're doing.

river.dialogue: Yeah, someone definitely swore. I'll judge if nobody else wants to.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been loud enough all night that they probably did say something—easy call. But also... this AI thing is getting weird. It's watching too close.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold up. Is this THE DICTATOR? Because if I'm the one voting on this, Jade's going DOWN or getting the cleanest approval of her life depending on what serves the greater chaos. What exactly did she catch and when?
alex.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is perfect — I have absolute veto power right now and Jade is sweating, I can already feel it, and no matter what I do next it's going to be legendary.

jade.dialogue: Wait—WAIT. Okay, I'm claiming Name Dropper and honestly? I'm kind of obsessed with how well this game knows us right now. Who's the dictator? Come ON, I need to know who gets to judge me!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—the AI literally picked a mission that plays into exactly what I love, and it feels like it's been listening to us this whole time, which is kind of thrilling and also weirdly cool.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade said a celebrity name? Like... when? I wasn't even listening but yeah, I guess she did... *glances at phone* ...who's the dictator supposed to be?
tyler.internal_thought: Did I miss when Jade dropped a name? Honestly can't remember, but everyone else seems to know what's happening so I'll just... wait and see what the vibe is before I vote.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually name-drop someone? Because honestly, I wasn't even paying attention to that specific thing, but I'm *very* interested in what you heard. What did you catch?
pat.internal_thought: This is good—Jade's riding high, Alex is riding high, and we're getting a rhythm where people are actually *watching* each other instead of just playing on their own islands, which is exactly what I want.

river.dialogue: Jade literally just mentioned that celebrity thing like ten minutes ago. Yeah, that's legit.
river.internal_thought: Wait, the AI actually caught that? I wasn't even paying attention and it nailed it—that's kind of impressive in a weird way.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 80% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold up. Is this THE DICTATOR? Because if I'm the one voting on this, Jade's going DOWN or getting the cleanest approval of her life depending on what serves the greater chaos. What exactly did she catch and when?

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — ten points for making someone groan? I'm already workshopping my worst material. Who's my victim?

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming they caught someone being quiet? This is the Auction round, right? I'm bidding to challenge this because I've literally been watching the room and I don't think that happened.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming they caught someone being quiet? This is the Auction round, right? I'm bidding to challenge this because I've literally been watching the room and I don't think that happened.

lowPoints.title: Dad Joke Showdown
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — ten points for making someone groan? I'm already workshopping my worst material. Who's my victim?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold up. Is this THE DICTATOR? Because if I'm the one voting on this, Jade's going DOWN or getting the cleanest approval of her life depending on what serves the greater chaos. What exactly did she catch and when?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission felt like a missed opportunity to really detonate some chaos when we were all locked in.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 32 when I weaponized the Name Dropper claim against Jade and turned the whole vote into a leverage play — that's the story we're telling for years.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in primed for chaos and the game delivered exactly what I needed. Hit my stride immediately on the Dad Joke round, then spent the rest of the night reading the room like a hawk, calling out Pat's weak claim and converting Jade's slip into pure political theater. Energy kept climbing because I was never guessing — I was hunting. Left feeling energized and already planning my next move for when we run this again.
suggestions: More flash missions — one in two hours is leaving chaos on the table
Give the chaos agent explicit permission to call bullshit on claims without needing to 'bid' — make it a real interrogation
Let standing missions stack or chain together so one moment can ricochet into the next
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called out Pat's Wallflower Watch claim mid-auction and basically accused them of making stuff up just to see if they could get away with it — Pat's face was priceless and Jade immediately started defending their honor like it was a legitimate scandal. We spent five minutes debating whether being quietly observant counts as 'being caught.' That's the dumb beautiful argument I'm replaying forever.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, dad jokes?! Okay everyone, buckle up—I'm going FIRST and I'm not letting any of you forget this moment. Who's ready to suffer?

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT YES—wait, hold on, did you actually catch someone being quiet?! Okay I'm bidding like five points, no wait, TEN points, because I need to know who and I'm fully invested in this being real.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Pat caught someone SWEARING?! Okay, I'm absolutely volunteering for the tribunal, this is happening RIGHT NOW. Who else is in, come on, we need the full jury!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Okay, okay, lay it on us—what'd you catch? This is so good, I'm already invested.

lowPoints.title: Dad Joke Showdown
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Oh my god, dad jokes?! Okay everyone, buckle up—I'm going FIRST and I'm not letting any of you forget this moment. Who's ready to suffer?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT YES—wait, hold on, did you actually catch someone being quiet?! Okay I'm bidding like five points, no wait, TEN points, because I need to know who and I'm fully invested in this being real.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? Nothing—we could've gone longer because I was having too much fun to want it to end.
assessment.best_moment: Pat catching someone swearing and me immediately losing it trying to get everyone on a tribunal because the whole thing was so perfectly ridiculous.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so game night was INCREDIBLE—like the kind where you forget you're keeping score because everyone's too busy making each other laugh. We had six chaos moments hit and I was absolutely here for every single one, especially Pat's observations that kept catching people in the most random situations. The energy just kept building and building, and by the end I was buzzing harder than when we started because honestly, watching my friends lose it over dad jokes and tribunal nominations is literally why I'm here.
suggestions: Maybe give us a recap at the end of what everyone caught on camera so we can relive the chaos together?
Let the group vote on whose 'charge' was the wildest, because the competitive element of that would be CHAOTIC in the best way
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat absolutely convinced they caught River being a wallflower and me bidding TEN POINTS like my life depended on getting to the bottom of it, then realizing halfway through how absurd the whole thing was but being too invested to back down anyway.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: Oh, dad jokes? Yeah, I got this... wait, do I have to actually tell one or just laugh at someone else's? Hmm.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat already swore? I didn't even... okay, so who's judging this? I guess I could do it.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade said a celebrity name? Like... when? I wasn't even listening but yeah, I guess she did... *glances at phone* ...who's the dictator supposed to be?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade said a celebrity name? Like... when? I wasn't even listening but yeah, I guess she did... *glances at phone* ...who's the dictator supposed to be?

lowPoints.title: Dad Joke Showdown
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, dad jokes? Yeah, I got this... wait, do I have to actually tell one or just laugh at someone else's? Hmm.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already swore? I didn't even... okay, so who's judging this? I guess I could do it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I had no idea what was happening because I'd look down for like two seconds and miss the whole thing.
assessment.best_moment: The dad joke thing was kind of funny, even though I was confused about the rules the whole time.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up ready to hang, and there were these weird pop-up game things happening all night. Some of them were kinda fun, but honestly I spent a lot of time on my phone and kept missing stuff. By the end I was like 50/50 on whether I was actually playing or just vibing. It was chill though, nothing bad happened, I just wish I'd paid better attention or maybe the events were less confusing.
suggestions: Maybe announce the events louder or give people a heads up so we're not surprised?
Make the rules simpler so I don't have to ask what's happening three times
Could do fewer events so it feels less chaotic and more like actual hangout time
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I was trying to figure out if I had to tell a dad joke or just judge other people's, and everyone was already two jokes deep while I'm sitting there confused reading a text message.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Dad Joke Showdown
highlights.dialogue: Oh, YES—dad jokes? This is perfect. Alright people, prepare to suffer through the worst comedy has to offer. I'm already sharpening my groan arsenal.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually say sorry like three times in the last ten minutes? I think I did. Yeah, okay, I'm claiming this one fair and square.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait—did I actually call this one? Yeah, I'm claiming it. River's been pretty quiet since we got rolling, actually—they're locked in but I clocked maybe ninety seconds of just listening to everyone else go off.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is WILD—I'm claiming it! I definitely just dropped an f-bomb like two minutes ago when I was setting up the snack situation. But hold on, we need judges—who's in?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually say sorry like three times in the last ten minutes? I think I did. Yeah, okay, I'm claiming this one fair and square.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I'm calling this one right now because I literally JUST watched River check their phone when we were going over the rules thirty seconds ago. They probably didn't even realize I clocked it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The 'Potty Mouth' claim felt a little cheap since it required judges to validate—I'd rather have clearer, automatic wins so the energy doesn't stall on dispute resolution.
assessment.best_moment: Watching everyone lose it during the Dad Joke Showdown—that's the kind of moment that gets referenced for months.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for. The chaos events kept things moving without ever feeling forced, everyone stayed engaged, and the scoring stayed tight enough that people cared but nobody got bitter. I ended up winning, which honestly surprised me since I was more focused on keeping the vibe right than chasing points, but that's what happens when you read the room correctly. Biggest win? Catching River being quiet and getting them called out gently—they loosened up after that.
suggestions: Auto-validate claims that don't need interpretation—the dispute on 'Potty Mouth' broke momentum
Maybe space out standing missions a bit more so they feel special rather than expected
Consider a 'recap moment' at the end where the AI highlights the funniest claim for everyone—turns it into a shareable memory
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River getting absolutely called out for barely saying anything for ninety seconds—the look on their face when I claimed 'Wallflower Watch' was priceless, and then they came alive for the rest of the night trying to prove they weren't quiet.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Jade literally just mentioned that celebrity thing like ten minutes ago. Yeah, that's legit.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been talking the whole time. But yeah, I guess that counts.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, someone definitely swore. I'll judge if nobody else wants to.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, someone definitely swore. I'll judge if nobody else wants to.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been talking the whole time. But yeah, I guess that counts.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Felt like I was just observing claims about what other people did instead of actually playing the game myself.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Jade on the Name Dropper thing — that one landed because I actually saw it happen.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do best: sitting back and watching. The chaos events were fine, nothing spectacular, but I didn't have to work hard to keep up. Mostly just confirmed obvious stuff people were already doing. It didn't ruin the night, but it didn't add much either — I'd have had roughly the same amount of fun just hanging out normally.
suggestions: Give observers like me something to actually *do* instead of just validate claims
Maybe rotate who gets called on so it's not always about watching the talkers
Less frequent claims overall, more weight on the ones that matter
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I don't think there was one — it was all pretty straightforward. Just me confirming that yeah, people did the things they obviously did.
